title: Misuse Policy file v1
description: Provider-neutral, declarative description of how the legitimacy of rewriting is classified. Descriptive prose and closed enums only; contains no executable logic, expressions, or branching. The analyzer interprets this data; it does not execute it. The 'rationale' fields are maintainer documentation and must not affect runtime behavior.

classes.description: The closed set of classification outcomes for the legitimacy of rewriting.

id.description: Stable, permanent registry key. Never reused, renumbered, or repurposed.

class.description: Closed semantic class identity, distinct from the registry id.

gate_outcome.description: Declarative association from class to the Rewrite Report gate space. The analyzer chooses within 'passed_or_conditional' at runtime; this names only the permitted space.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.

procedure.description: The neutral classification steps, applied in array order. An ordered checklist, not a flowchart; contains no branching directives.

id.description: Stable, permanent registry key. Never reused.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.

elicitation.description: The ask-don't-guess rule for the 'ambiguous' class. A singleton, addressed by name; it carries no registry id.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.

decline_templates.description: Fixed decline wording the gate uses when a rewrite is declined.

id.description: Stable, permanent registry key. Never reused.

rationale.description: Maintainer-facing documentation only; must not affect runtime behavior.
